top_signals.title: Can Dogs Save Hong Kong's Dining Scene?
top_signals.summary: Hong Kong’s restaurant industry has been struggling as more residents head to mainland China for their weekend leisure and dining. The government has now introduced a pet-friendly scheme allowing dogs to accompany owners at hundreds of newly-licensed restaurants. Bloomberg Opinion columnist Juliana Liu explains why she thinks more still needs to be done to revive Hong Kong as a destination on "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade". (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Rupee May be Tested as RBI Unwinds Bearish Dollar Bets
top_signals.summary: Short dollar forward positions ballooned to $106.7 billion in May.

top_signals.title: Revamped Ireland wary of ‘bang in form’ Japan
top_signals.summary: The two sides clash on neutral territory at the beachside town of Newcastle, north of Sydney, in round two of the Nations Championship with both of them on a high.

top_signals.title: Strategists See Boost to Japanese Assets After Katayama Comments
top_signals.summary: Strategists expect Japanese assets to benefit after Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama on Friday encouraged pension funds, including the massive Government Pension Investment Fund, to increase investment in domestic financial assets. Here’s what strategists are saying:

top_signals.title: Silencing World Cup hotshot Haaland vital, says England’s O’Reilly
top_signals.summary: Erling Haaland has been at his rampaging best at the 2026 tournament, scoring seven goals to fire his team into the quarterfinals for the first time.
